channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: DBS, Southeast Asia’s largest bank, partners with VC giant Granite Asia to counter the region’s lack of capital
item.description: The partnership launches with a new $110 million fund, exclusively for DBS's wealth clients, that targets IPO-stage AI and tech startups in the region...

item.title: Why are some people better at multitasking?
item.description: Our capacity to juggle several tasks at once is among the most important capabilities of the human cognitive system. Just consider a typical day in th...

item.title: CEOs who despised Trump’s tariffs are still silent after Supreme Court ruling: ‘There’s no upside in speaking up’
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: How an ‘Infinity Mirror’ Device Could Help Solve AI’s Biggest Problem
item.description: Artificial intelligence companies are spending billions on chips and infrastructure. Researchers think optical computing could help.

item.title: Wall Street Is Done Paying a Premium for Software Stocks
item.description: The AI trade has changed dramatically in a matter of weeks.

item.title: U.S. asks citizens in Mexico to shelter after cartel leader killed
item.description: The security alert covers the states of Jalisco, Tamaulipas, Michoacán, Guerrero and Nuevo León.

item.title: Delta expects to halt flights at NYC, Boston airports for storm
item.description: More than 8,500 flights have been canceled through Tuesday, according to tracking site FlightAware.

item.title: Jeff Bezos Says You Shouldn’t Compromise When You Make Decisions. Science Agrees
item.description: Especially if your goal is to avoid sub-optimal outcomes.

item.title: 9 Questions That Reveal the Truth About How Employees See Their Boss
item.description: The answers will tell you whether you’re building trust and engagement or quietly fueling turnover.

item.title: Employers love tricky job interview questions, but they’re actually useless
item.description: If you have ever interviewed for a job, there is a non-trivial probability that you have encountered “tricky” or quirky interview questions. These are...

item.title: This Is the Ideal Number of Cups of Coffee a Day to Protect Brain Health
item.description: A huge new study pinpoints the exact amount of coffee to maximize health benefits and minimize sleep problems.

item.title: Why are Europeans eating more plant-based meat than Americans? It’s not why you think
item.description: If you walk into a grocery store in the Netherlands or Germany, you might not realize you’re being steered toward plant-based protein, from vegan tort...

item.title: How to build team culture that sticks
item.description: Corporate culture isn’t built by policies. It’s built by moments—the unscripted experiences that catch us off guard, bring us closer, and quietly shap...

item.title: Trump boasts that the U.S. has ‘the greatest economy actually ever.’ Human nature suggests that’s exactly the wrong message to send to voters
item.description: History suggests that voters' anxiety can outweigh good economic numbers at the polls. Just ask George Bush and Bill Clinton.

item.title: The leadership skills AI can’t replace
item.description: A CEO sits in a boardroom, staring at a strategy deck generated overnight by AI . The analysis is sharp. The recommendations are confident. The number...

item.title: Tariff costs and refunds take the spotlight as retailers set to report earnings
Novo Nordisk says new weight-loss drug not as potent as Eli Lilly's; stock sinks
Gold sheds its safe-haven status. Is it just another momentum play now?
U.S. stock futures flat as investors await clarity on Trump’s tariff plans
Could these 6 non-AI chip stocks be the next leg of the AI boom?
